methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Aeon Industrial is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $18.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms operating in the same sector and stage, including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $18.6M raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $46M. Peer-Median analysis examines four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The median valuation across this set is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

The two methods produce materially different indications. Synthesizing across both approaches yields a triangulated range of $40M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $136M. The wide dispersion reflects tension between stage-based progression from deployed capital and peer comparables that span Series A valuations significantly above typical Seed outcomes. Key uncertainties include whether comparable companies' higher valuations reflect sector-specific dynamics or outlier fundraising rather than intrinsic value, and whether Aeon's technology differentiation justifies positioning toward the upper or lower quartile of the peer range.
